requirements.title: Stack Initialization
requirements.description: The stack module shall initialize from a caller-provided backing array.
requirements.rationale: LIFO stacks are essential for undo buffers, expression evaluation, and depth-first traversal in embedded systems; initializing from a caller-provided array satisfies the zero-allocation constraint.

requirements.title: Stack Initial State
requirements.description: The stack shall set length to zero on initialization.
requirements.rationale: Zeroing the length on initialization establishes a known-empty state, ensuring the stack is immediately usable without undefined behavior from uninitialized fields.

requirements.title: Stack Push
requirements.description: The stack shall copy an element to the top in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) push provides deterministic timing for real-time producers.

requirements.title: Stack Full Rejection
requirements.description: The stack shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_SIZE_ERROR when push is attempted on a full stack.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on a full stack surfaces the overflow condition to the caller rather than silently corrupting adjacent memory.

requirements.title: Stack Pop
requirements.description: The stack shall remove and copy out the top element in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: O(1) pop provides deterministic timing for real-time consumers.

requirements.title: Stack Empty Pop Rejection
requirements.description: The stack shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_SIZE_ERROR when pop is attempted on an empty stack.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on an empty stack surfaces the underflow condition rather than returning stale or uninitialized data.

requirements.title: Stack Peek
requirements.description: The stack shall return a pointer descriptor to the top element without removing it in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: Peek allows consumers to inspect the top element without consuming it, which is useful for lookahead and conditional pop logic.

requirements.title: Stack Empty Peek Rejection
requirements.description: The stack shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_SIZE_ERROR when peek is attempted on an empty stack.
requirements.rationale: Explicit rejection on empty peek prevents the caller from receiving a descriptor to uninitialized or stale memory.
